action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 9 trained an ensemble of 5 models with heavy regularization (dropout=0.4, weight_decay=1e-3) and achieved AUC=0.7543, below the best single-model score of 0.7594 from iteration 6. The individual ensemble models had val AUCs around 0.747-0.749, indicating too much regularization caused underfitting. Analysis: The current architecture (2-layer LSTM, combined embeddings, LayerNorm) is solid, but dropout=0.4 + weight_decay=1e-3 suppresses performance. By reducing regularization (dropout=0.2, weight_decay=0) and training longer with patience=12, the model should fit better and surpass 0.7594. If this single run succeeds, I can scale to an ensemble in the next iteration. Decision: Run train.py with reduced regularization and longer training, then immediately submit eval on the resulting best_model.pt.
